channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Australia’s cautious alignment with US actions on Iran raises concerns over international law
item.description: The article examines the potential risks of Australia aligning too closely with the United States amid escalating tensions with Iran. Zoe Daniel highlights concerns about Prime Minister Anthony Albane...

item.title: Lagos State Government Issues Warning to Actress Sarah Martins Over Street Cooking for Charity
item.description: The Lagos State Government don warn Nollywood actress Sarah Martins say make she stop to cook and share food for public roads inside the state. Commissioner for Environment and Water Resources, Tokunb...

item.title: UK Government to Operate Charter Flight from Dubai for British Nationals Amid Regional Conflict
item.description: The UK government has announced plans to operate a charter flight from Dubai early next week to assist British nationals wishing to leave the region, depending on the local security situation. The fli...

item.title: James Orengo Calls for Official Inquiry Into Raila Odinga's Passing
item.description: On Sunday, March 8, Kenyan newspapers reported extensively on multiple issues, notably the severe flooding in Nairobi, which prompted President William Ruto to intervene. Besides the natural disaster,...

item.title: MOI Implements Expansion Projects to Reduce Surgery Waiting Times
item.description: Dodoma – Muhimbili Orthopaedic Institute (MOI) in Dar es Salaam explain say long waiting times for surgery are mostly because of limited theatre space and big increase in trauma cases. MOI Executive D...

item.title: Julian Barnes Concludes His Literary Journey with ‘Departure(s)’
item.description: Julian Barnes, the 2011 Booker Prize-winning author, has released his fifteenth and final novel, 'Departure(s)', marking the end of a remarkable literary career. At 80 years old, Barnes presents a nov...

item.title: Hennie du Plessis Leads Joburg Open After Marathon 27-Hole Effort
item.description: South Africa’s Hennie du Plessis has taken the lead at the Joburg Open after a demanding day on the course, completing a total of 27 holes on Saturday. Du Plessis had to finish the suspended second ro...

item.title: Tanzania progresses steadily with AFCON 2027 stadium preparations
item.description: The Tanzanian government has confirmed that preparations for hosting the Africa Cup of Nations (AFCON) 2027 are moving along well. Deputy Minister Hamis Mwinjuma visited the Benjamin Mkapa and Uhuru S...

item.title: Democrats Assess Opportunity in Texas’ 23rd District After Gonzales Withdraws
item.description: Texas’ 23rd Congressional District may become unexpectedly competitive following Rep. Tony Gonzales’ decision to withdraw from his reelection campaign amid a personal scandal. Gonzales’ exit has left ...

item.title: Police Investigate Overnight Shooting at Two Synagogues in North York and Thornhill
item.description: Overnight, two synagogues located in North York and Thornhill were targeted in separate shooting incidents. Police confirmed that shots were fired at both places of worship, prompting immediate invest...

item.title: Nigerian Army Rescues 19 Kidnapped Residents in Abuja, Neutralises Bandit
item.description: Today for Gidan Dogo, Bwari Area Council inside Abuja, Nigerian Army don carry out one well-coordinated operation wey rescue 19 people wey bandits don kidnap recently. The operation na result of corre...

item.title: U.S. Customs and Border Protection Purchased Phone Location Data from Online Ads
item.description: The United States Customs and Border Protection (CBP) has admitted to purchasing phone location data from the online advertising industry, marking a significant expansion of government surveillance ca...

item.title: Bronson Garlick Makes Memorable NRL Debut for South Sydney Rabbitohs
item.description: Bronson Garlick experienced a dream come true when he made his debut for the South Sydney Rabbitohs at hooker against the Dolphins at Suncorp Stadium. The moment held particular significance as Bronso...

item.title: Two Siblings Killed in Motorcycle-Van Collision in Maguindanao
item.description: In a tragic accident in Maguindanao del Sur, a 16-year-old high school student, Norsiya Saban, and her 8-year-old brother, Suhod, lost their lives after their motorcycle collided with a delivery van i...

item.title: How Regular Eating of Instant Noodles Fit Affect Your Health
item.description: Instant noodles na popular quick meal for students, busy workers, and people wey wan save small money. Dem cheap, dem fill belle, and e dey everywhere for hostels, offices, shops, and supermarkets. Bu...

item.title: Trump’s Use of ‘Unconditional Surrender’ Raises Questions About U.S. Strategy in Iran
item.description: Former U.S. President Donald Trump recently invoked the term “unconditional surrender” in reference to the ongoing conflict with Iran, a phrase historically tied to decisive military victory and compr...

item.title: Ezekiel Mutua Joins Friends to Attend Bolo Bespoke’s Wife’s Thanksgiving Ceremony in Ukambani
item.description: On 7th March 2026, Bolo Bespoke and his wife Mueni hosted a thanksgiving ceremony at her family home in Ukambani, and it was a star-studded affair. The couple looked stunning, with Mueni rocking a bro...

item.title: Murang'a family seeks answers over man who died hours after encounter with police and suspected goons
item.description: This article narrates the troubling case of a Murang'a man who died only a few hours after he is said to have met police officers and a group of suspected goons in unclear circumstances. The story foc...

item.title: Two mobile phones seized from parcel intended for inmate in Doda jail
item.description: In Jammu and Kashmir’s Doda district, authorities recovered two mobile phones during a routine inspection of a parcel meant for an inmate at Bhaderwah Jail on Friday. The inmate, identified as Irshad ...
